title: Array (basics) Largest/ second smallest / sorted / remove duplicates
content:
DSA

📌 PHASE 1 — ARRAY BASICS & LARGEST ELEMENT

🔹 What is Array (DS)?

Array is a DS (Data Structure) which store similar element.

Agar yeh DS ek particular type ka hai

toh similar type hona chahiye

chahe integer ho, char ho, string ho, pair ho.

Array looks like

[ ][ ][ ]

🔹 Declaration of Array

In C / C++

cpp
int arr[6];

In Java

java
int arr[] =newarr[6];

jab aap yeh likh dete ho toh kya hota hai ki

size 6 ka array create hota hai

  • agar inside main declare kiya → garbage values aati hain
  • agar global declare kiya → 0 se filled hota hai (C++)

Java me bhi default value 0 hoti hai


ek baar inside kiya tha store ek baar outside dekho values kitni alag h 

Python ka sure nahi 😅

📝 NOTE (Correction)

  • Java me bhi garbage values nahi hoti, default value 0 hoti hai
  • Python me traditional array nahi hota, list hoti hai (dynamic)

🔹 Maximum Size of Array

  • Inside int main() → 10^6
  • Global array → 10^7

📝 NOTE (Correction)

Yeh stack vs heap limitation ki wajah se hota hai.

Global arrays heap/static memory me jaate hain.

🔹 Accessing an Array

Array ko access karne ke liye index use hota hai.

  • index starts from 0 to n-1
cpp
for(int i =0; i < n; i++) {
print(arr[i]);
}

🔹 Where is Array Stored in Memory?

Array computer memory me store hota hai.

  • maan lo array ka first block random x location pe hai
  • hume x pata hota hai
  • 0th index → x
  • 1st index → x + 1
  • 2nd index → x + 2

Array continuous memory me store hota hai.

🔥 LARGEST ELEMENT IN AN ARRAY

Hum direct solution nahi denge,

interview ko drive karna hai

Flow hoga:

👉 Brute → Better → Optimal

Example

arr[] = [3, 2, 1, 5, 2]

🔹 Brute Force Approach

Idea:

  • Array ko sort karo
  • Last element pick karo
cpp
largest = arr[n-1];

Complexity

  • Sorting → O(n log n)
  • Space → O(1) (kuch naya nahi bana rahe)

🔹 Optimal Approach

Idea:

  • First element ko largest maan lo
  • Traverse karo
  • Agar koi bada mil jaye → largest update
cpp
largest = arr[0];

for(int i =0; i < n; i++) {
if(arr[i] > largest) {
        largest = arr[i];
    }
}

print(largest);

Complexity

  • Time: O(n)
  • Optimise ho gaya 🎯

👉 Next Phase me cover karenge:

  • Second Largest
  • Second Smallest
  • Check if array sorted
  • Remove duplicates (Brute → Optimal)

Bolo Phase 2 chalu karein? 💪

next

Perfect 👍

👉 PHASE 2: Second Largest, Second Smallest, Check Sorted Array

(Again reminder: aapke words, language, flow same rakhe gaye hain, bas structure + spelling + syntax clean kiya hai.

Galat cheez ke niche NOTE diya hai.)

Selection sort mein previous pass ka smallest next pass mein use nahi karte.

📌 PHASE 2 — SECOND LARGEST / SECOND SMALLEST / CHECK SORTED

🔥 SECOND LARGEST ELEMENT IN ARRAY

Interview me second largest ya second smallest dono puch sakte hain.

🔹 Brute Force Approach

Idea:

  • Array ko sort kar do
  • arr[n-2] output hoga

Par dikkat yeh hai 👇

kya pata saare element same ho

ya last ke 4–5 same ho

Example:

1 2 4 5 7 7
  • largest = arr[n-1]
  • n-1 se peeche traverse karo
  • jo largest ke barabar nahi ho → wahi second largest
cpp
largest = arr[n-1];

for(int i = n-2; i >=0; i--) {
if(arr[i] != largest) {
        secondLargest = arr[i];
break;// necessary kyuki aage ka koi fayda nahi
    }
}

Worst Case

1 7 7 7 7 7
  • poora traverse karna padega

Complexity

  • Sorting → O(n log n)
  • Loop → O(n)
  • Total → O(n log n + n)

🔹 Better Approach (2 Pass)

Idea:

  1. First pass → largest nikaalo
  2. Second pass → second largest nikaalo

Example:

1 2 4 7 7 5
  • largest = 7
  • secondLargest = -1 (maan liya array me positives hain)

Condition:

  • element largest se chota ho
  • element secondLargest se bada ho
cpp
largest = arr[0];

// First pass
for(int i =0; i < n; i++) {
if(arr[i] > largest) {
        largest = arr[i];
    }
}

secondLargest =-1;

// Second pass
for(int i =0; i < n; i++) {
if(arr[i] > secondLargest && arr[i] != largest) {
        secondLargest = arr[i];
    }
}

Complexity

  • First pass → O(n)
  • Second pass → O(n)
  • Total → O(2n) ≈ O(n)

🔹 Optimal Approach (Single Pass)

Idea:

  • largest aur secondLargest dono saath manage karo

Example:

1, 2, 4, 7, 7, 5

Steps:

  • first element → largest
  • secondLargest ko bahut chota maan lo

Logic:

  • agar koi element largest se bada ho

    → secondLargest = largest

    → largest = current element

  • agar element largest se chota ho

    aur secondLargest se bada ho

    → secondLargest update

cpp
largest = arr[0];
slargest =-1;// agar negative values ho sakti hain toh INT_MIN

for(int i =1; i < n; i++) {
if(arr[i] > largest) {
        slargest = largest;
        largest = arr[i];
    }
elseif(arr[i] < largest && arr[i] > slargest) {
        slargest = arr[i];
    }
}

return slargest;

Example:

8 7 5 6 7 8 9

🔥 SECOND SMALLEST ELEMENT

Same logic bas ulta.

  • smallest = first element
  • ssmallest = INT_MAX
cpp
smallest = arr[0];
ssmallest = INT_MAX;

for(int i =1; i < n; i++) {
if(arr[i] < smallest) {
        ssmallest = smallest;
        smallest = arr[i];
    }
elseif(arr[i] > smallest && arr[i] < ssmallest) {
        ssmallest = arr[i];
    }
}

Complexity

  • Time: O(n)
  • Single pass
  • Better than better solution 😎

🔍 CHECK IF ARRAY IS SORTED

Example:

[1, 2, 3, 3, 4] → sorted
[1, 2, 1, 3, 4] →not sorted

Isme brute / better / optimal ki koi zarurat nahi

Simple logic hai.

Idea:

  • har element ko previous se compare karo
  • agar kahin bhi chota mil gaya → not sorted
cpp
for(int i =1; i < n; i++) {
if(arr[i] >= arr[i-1]) {
// kuch nahi karo
    }
else {
returnfalse;
    }
}

returntrue;

Explanation

  • agar loop poora chal gaya

    matlab koi dikkat nahi aayi

  • isliye last me return true

👉 Next Phase me aayega:

  • Remove duplicates from sorted array
  • Brute → Optimal (Two Pointer)

📌 PHASE 3 — REMOVE DUPLICATES (IN-PLACE)

🔥 Problem Statement

Sorted array diya hai, duplicates remove karne hain in-place.

Example:

arr = [1,1,2,2,2,3,3]

Output:

arr = [1,2,3, _, _, _, _]
  • first unique elements starting me aane chahiye
  • baaki jagah kuch bhi ho sakta hai
  • new array nahi banana
  • same array use karna hai

Agar 3 unique elements hain

toh starting ke 3 index pe hi hone chahiye.

🔹 Brute Force Approach

Idea:

“unique chahiye mujhe”

mere dimag me set aa raha hai

  • set duplicate allow nahi karta
  • array traverse karo
  • set me insert karo
cpp
set<int> st;

for(int i =0; i < n; i++) {
    st.insert(arr[i]);
}

Ab set me sirf unique elements hain.

Ab wapas array me daalna hai 👇

cpp
int index =0;
for(auto it : st) {
    arr[index] = it;
    index++;
}

Complexity

  • set insertion → O(n log n)
  • array overwrite → O(n)
  • total → O(n log n + n)
  • space → O(n) (extra set use hua)

📝 NOTE

  • Problem ne bola tha in-place,

    lekin yahan extra space use ho rahi hai

    isliye interview me yeh optimal nahi maana jaata

🔹 Optimal Approach (Two Pointer)

Yeh important interview solution hai 🔥

Given:

arr = [1,1,2,2,2,3,3]

Idea (aapke words me):

  • array sorted hai (bahut important)
  • ek pointer hoga jo unique element ko point karega
  • dusra pointer aage jaa ke naya element dhundega

jo first pointer hai

wo initially arr[0] pe hoga

aur wo dhundega ki

“mere jaisa element kaha nahi mil raha”

Jaha different element milega

waha usko next position pe daal denge

Two Pointer Explanation

  • i → unique element ka index
  • j → traversal ke liye

Start:

i =0

Loop:

cpp
for(int j =1; j < n; j++) {
if(arr[j] != arr[i]) {
        i++;
        arr[i] = arr[j];
    }
}

Final Result

  • array ke 0 se i tak → unique elements
  • total unique elements = i + 1

Example Dry Run

arr = [1,1,2,2,2,3,3]
i =0
  • j=1 → same (1==1) → ignore
  • j=2 → different (2!=1) → i=1 → arr[1]=2
  • j=3 → same → ignore
  • j=4 → same → ignore
  • j=5 → different → i=2 → arr[2]=3
  • j=6 → same → ignore

Final array:

[1, 2, 3, x, x, x, x]

Unique count:

i +1 =3

🔹 Complexity (Optimal)

  • Time: O(n)
  • Space: O(1)
  • Single pass
  • Fully in-place

🔥 This is the best solution

✅ Summary (Interview Friendly)

  • Sorted array hai → Two Pointer
  • Extra space allowed → set
  • In-place chahiye → optimal solution use karo
